name: New Mexican Pork and Green Chili Stew
recipeIngredient: 4 1/2 pounds boneless pork shoulder, trimmed of excess and cut into 1-inch pieces
5 pounds frozen roasted new mexican green chilies, thawed, peeled if necessary, seeded, and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large heavy kettle, cook the onions and 6 of the garlic cloves, minced, in oil over moderate heat, stirring until the onions are softened.
2. Add the pork, chilies, water, and salt; simmer the mixture uncovered, adding more water if necessary to keep the pork barely covered, for 1 1/2 hours.
3. Stir in the potatoes, peeled and cut into 1-inch pieces, making sure they are covered by the cooking liquid.
4. Simmer the mixture stirring occasionally for 30 minutes, or until the pork and potatoes are tender.
5. Stir in the remaining 2 garlic cloves, minced, and salt to taste; simmer the stew for 5 minutes.
The stew may be frozen or made up to 3 days in advance. Cool, uncovered, then keep covered and chilled.
